## [Note]

dbbot v0.10.0 promotes the local support matrix into the MySQL package preparation path.

The MySQL deployment entry playbooks now use `dbbotctl support` as the source of truth for supported server package names, download URLs, and available checksums. This keeps package rules in one managed matrix instead of duplicating package naming logic inside playbook variables.

## [Add]

- Add `dbbotctl support` commands for support matrix listing, package rule queries, package checksum verification, and matrix validation.
- Add local support matrix data for MySQL, Percona Server, GreatSQL, and ClickHouse package rules.
- Add MySQL package metadata resolution from `dbbotctl support` before package preparation.
- Add MySQL server package checksum verification for automatic downloads and local package pre-checks when checksum metadata is available.

## [Change]

- Use support matrix package metadata for MySQL, Percona Server, and GreatSQL server package names and download URLs.
- Replace playbook-side MySQL server package naming defaults with runtime package metadata resolved from `dbbotctl support`.
- Update MySQL package documentation to describe support matrix based package resolution and checksum verification.

## [Fix]

- Fail earlier when a requested MySQL package rule is unsupported, ambiguous, missing, or checksum-mismatched.
